license_or_terms_summary: The Odds API terms (reviewed 2026-06-11) permit using odds data in analytical tools, dashboards, and user-facing applications, including commercial use, provided the data is not resold, repackaged, or redistributed as a standalone data product (no raw feeds, downloadable files, or derivative data services). Provider names may be referenced factually without implying endorsement.
derived_storage: data/processed/odds_2026.parquet (gitignored, regenerated from code); only de-margined benchmark probabilities and aggregate comparisons appear in committed reports and notebooks
decision: The Odds API v4 is selected as the World Cup 2026 odds provider. An unauthenticated capability probe on 2026-06-11 confirmed the sports endpoint is live and key-gated (HTTP 401, error_code MISSING_KEY), so a personal ODDS_API_KEY is required and must live only in the ignored local .env. Terms permit analytical benchmark use without redistributing raw payloads, therefore raw provider responses are stored only under the gitignored data/raw/odds/ path. The active World Cup 2026 sport_key is intentionally null here: it is resolved at runtime by probe_odds_provider once the credential exists, never hard-coded. The authored CSV template data/external/odds_2026_template.csv remains the manual import fallback if the provider tier lacks World Cup three-way markets.
